source_of_truth: draft.md contains manuscript prose; this registry is an audit and navigation layer.

objects.description: Simultaneous impact-only observed residual model u_t = B0 epsilon_t + eta_t with unit-variance structural shocks and additive residual noise; VAR lags and dynamic responses are outside the first paper.
For the robust transformed-cumulant route, additive residual noise is independent of structural shocks and Gaussian, so every linear transform of the noise has zero cumulants above order two.

Scale must be fixed once, not twice. M0034 flags that the manuscript cannot simultaneously use the diagonal-normalized chart diag(B)=1 and also impose unit shock variances when deriving second-order restrictions. The revised robust-DW object should either use a unit-variance impact scale, profile shock variances in a diagonal-normalized chart, or avoid second-order anchors.
Population sign-restricted set built from a covariance factor P_* P_*' = B0 B0' + V and restrictions R(P_* Q) >= 0.
objects.source: vault/syntheses/Noisy residuals in recursive and sign-restricted SVARs.md

objects.description: If V is nonzero, the standard sign-restricted population set is based on B0 B0' + V and generally differs from the no-noise economic sign set.
objects.source: vault/syntheses/Noisy residuals in recursive and sign-restricted SVARs.md

objects.description: B0(D^2 - I)B0' = V, equivalently D^2 - I = B0^{-1} V B0^{-1'}, showing why positive column rescaling generally cannot absorb additive residual noise.
objects.source: vault/syntheses/Noisy residuals in recursive and sign-restricted SVARs.md

objects.description: M27 common-chart reported standard-DW set: sign-admissible normalized candidates whose standardized recovered shocks pass a J-test stack containing the no-noise covariance moment and finite higher-moment independence restrictions.
objects.source: vault/papers/Refining set-identification in VARs through independence.md; manuscript/derivations/dw-robust-comparison-diagnostic.md

objects.description: M25 working result: under residual noise, standard DW recovered shocks are not structural shocks. A rich covariance-whitened J-test inversion is generically empty in population, except for structural-coordinate rescaling cases or finite-moment aliases; finite samples can produce falsely small least-rejected regions around pseudo-candidates.
objects.proof_status: M25 working derivation complete; needs audit and early Monte Carlo triage

objects.description: Population and sample J-test inversion for standard covariance-whitened DW candidates: g_infty(Q)'Wg_infty(Q) and T ghat(Q)'What ghat(Q), with asymptotic rejection when the population criterion is bounded away from zero.
objects.verification_status: M25 working derivation complete; needs early MC and audit before draft theorem wording

objects.description: Robust DW-style sign-restricted candidate set that searches over a normalized impact space and uses mixed higher cumulants of B^{-1}u rather than imposing no-noise recovered-shock covariance. The M0030 off-diagonal residual covariance anchor is superseded after M0034 because it double-normalizes scale in the diag(B)=1 chart unless unit shock variances are separately imposed.
objects.verification_status: M24 conditional audit passed for the pure local normalized Gaussian-noise higher-cumulant route. M0030/M37 added and audited a six-moment diagonal-anchor variant, but M0034 supersedes that variant: under diag(B)=1 the off-diagonal covariance is Sigma_u,12=b21*sigma_1^2+b12*sigma_2^2 unless unit shock variances are imposed as an extra scale normalization. M39 must rebuild the evidence around the pure five-moment route or a valid explicit scale model.

objects.description: Moment-equation representation of the robust DW stack after M0034: third mixed central moments and fourth mixed cumulants of z(B)=B^{-1}u, with transformed second moments entering only as covariance products inside fourth cumulants. The off-diagonal residual covariance anchor is no longer part of the active robust stack under diag(B)=1.
objects.verification_status: M24 algebra review passed for centered third moments and fourth cumulants with covariance-product subtractions. M0034 invalidated the added M0030 diagonal-noise covariance anchor under the active scale normalization, so the active pure stack has five moments and uses chi2_5 only as a pointwise guide pending M39 evidence rebuild.

objects.description: M0035 optional covariance screen: for B=[[1,a],[b,1]] and e(B)=B^{-1}u, diagonal residual noise V=diag(nu_1,nu_2) implies E[e1 e2]=(-b*nu_1-a*nu_2)/(1-ab)^2 at the candidate truth. With 0 <= nu_i <= 0.5, the sample recovered covariance must lie in the induced candidate-specific interval.
objects.verification_status: M0035 figure diagnostic generated; algebra and finite-sample interpretation still need M40 adversarial audit before theorem-level prose or M28/M29 rebuild.

objects.description: Working result: under additive Gaussian residual noise, mixed higher cumulants of B^{-1}u are not shifted by noise. In the bivariate normalized system, local higher-cumulant identification follows when each structural shock has a nonzero third or fourth cumulant. M0035 adds a candidate bounded-noise recovered-covariance screen as optional extra identifying information.
objects.proof_status: Pure higher-cumulant local-rank audit passed conditionally in M24. M0030/M37 diagonal-anchor pass judgment is superseded by M0034 scale correction. M0035 bounded screen generated a promising figure but needs M40 audit before draft theorem wording.

objects.description: Diagnostic statement to revise after M0034: standard-DW accepted mass outside a valid pure robust-DW set warns that no-noise covariance-target precision may be driven by residual-noise misspecification, while a large pure robust-DW set records the information lost by dropping invalid second-order restrictions.
objects.proof_status: M27 formalization complete for the older comparison metric, but M0034 supersedes the diagonal-anchor robust set used in M29. Recompute metrics after M39.

objects.description: M27 overlap metric bundle for the common normalized chart: accepted shares, symmetric Jaccard overlap, directional containment q_{S|R}=mu(S cap R)/mu(S), and warning share d_{S not subset R}=1-q_{S|R}.
Intuitive sign-set geometry is now covered by the top row of the selected M0030 residual-noise grid, which shows sign/covariance accepted sets moving as residual noise increases.
objects.output_source: Selected visual spine: manuscript/simulations/sign_dw_robust_noise_grid_figure.py and manuscript/figures/fig_sign_dw_robust_noise_grid.png. A separate covariance-ellipse panel is optional, not required for the first draft.

objects.description: Residual-noise story figure candidate after M0035. The bounded-noise robust row uses pure higher-cumulant J inversion plus a 0 <= nu_i <= 0.5 recovered-covariance feasibility screen; in the high-noise column standard DW rejects true B0 while bounded robust DW contains it with much smaller accepted share than the pure row.
objects.output_source: M0035 bounded variant: manuscript/simulations/sign_dw_robust_noise_grid_figure.py --robust-mode bounded, manuscript/simulations/sign_dw_bounded_noise_robust_grid_figure.md, and manuscript/figures/fig_sign_dw_bounded_noise_robust_grid.png. M0034 pure variant remains manuscript/figures/fig_sign_dw_pure_robust_noise_grid.png. The earlier diagonal-anchor output manuscript/figures/fig_sign_dw_robust_noise_grid.png is superseded for the robust row.

objects.description: Grid-pair story candidate after M0035. The valid robust comparison may use pure higher cumulants plus an explicit bounded-noise covariance screen; this must be audited before replacing the evidence spine.
objects.output_source: M0035 bounded Figure 1 variant: manuscript/figures/fig_sign_dw_bounded_noise_robust_grid.png and manuscript/simulations/sign_dw_bounded_noise_robust_grid_figure.md. Existing M0030/M28/M29 outputs are superseded for the robust row and must be rebuilt after M40 audit.

objects.description: Monte Carlo summary must be rebuilt after M0034 because the M29 robust row used the superseded diagonal-anchor statistic. The revised table should compare standard DW with the pure higher-cumulant robust set or another explicitly valid scale model.
objects.output_source: Superseded M29 output: manuscript/simulations/m29_calibrated_monte_carlo.py, manuscript/simulations/m29_calibrated_monte_carlo.md, manuscript/simulations/output/m29_calibrated_monte_carlo.json. Rebuild after M39.

objects.description: M24 adversarial review of the robust DW higher-cumulant derivation. Conditional pass: cumulant-to-moment algebra, second-moment exclusion, normalization caveat, local rank, Gaussian-noise condition, and bias language are recorded. M37 later audited the post-M0030 diagonal-noise covariance anchor as a separate six-moment estimator gate.

Direct post-M0030 audit of the reported diagonal-noise robust DW estimator. Superseded by M0034: the off-diagonal covariance anchor is not valid in the diag(B)=1 chart unless unit shock variances are also imposed. The pure higher-cumulant part remains the active valid route under independent Gaussian residual noise.

Adversarial review of simulation design and interpretation for noisy sign-set bias, standard DW false sharpening, and robust DW comparison. M30 completed the first audit layer for the M35 triage script: conditional pass for exploratory use only; population-grid and final Monte Carlo audits remain.

Refreshed M28 validation gate for the M0030 revised grid pair. Exact population moment diagnostics support robust-DW truth zeros under diagonal Gaussian residual noise, standard-DW divergence under noisy covariance targets, and weak/Gaussian higher-moment widening toward the covariance anchor. Repeated finite-sample seeds support the high-noise visual story at pointwise chi-square cutoffs, while M29 supplies calibrated evidence.

open_audits.question: Does the M25 standard-DW J-test derivation survive audit, especially the rich-stack generic emptying claim, structural-rescaling exception, and finite-moment alias caveats?
